title: Supporting the designation of March as National CHamoru Heritage and Culture Month.
plain_summary: This bill wants to make March a special month to celebrate CHamoru people. The CHamoru are the native people who originally lived on the islands of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands. The bill honors their history, culture, and the brave military service they provided to the United States, especially during World War Two. It recognizes all the important ways CHamoru people have helped America.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Resolution Supporting the designation of March as National CHamoru Heritage and Culture Month
sections.text: This resolution supports making March a month to celebrate CHamoru heritage and culture across the United States. The CHamoru are the native people who originally lived on the islands of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands. The first people arrived on these islands thousands of years ago and became skilled at traveling across the ocean. The CHamoru people call themselves i Taotao Tano' yan i Taotao Tasi, which means the people of the land and sea. Their culture is based on inafa' maolek, which focuses on showing respect, being generous, helping each other, and taking care of family. For hundreds of years, Guam and the Mariana Islands have been important stopping points for people crossing the Pacific Ocean. During World War Two, Japan took control of these islands and the CHamoru people lived under Japanese rule. The CHamoru people helped the United States win important battles at Guam, Saipan, and Tinian during the war. The United States freed Guam from Japanese control in July nineteen forty four after the second Battle of Guam. Today, more than two hundred thousand people living in Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the mainland United States have CHamoru ancestors. CHamoru men and women join the United States military at rates higher than the national average. The CHamoru people have shown great strength and determination throughout their history. Therefore, the House of Representatives recognizes the important contributions and achievements of the CHamoru people and encourages all Americans to observe National CHamoru Heritage and Culture Month.
